Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx

Class FlixelObject

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelDebugDrawable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
Direct Known Subclasses:
FlixelSprite
public class FlixelObject extends FlixelBasic implements FlixelDebugDrawable
The base class for all visual/spatial objects in Flixel. Extends FlixelBasic with position (x, y), dimensions (width, height), rotation (angle), and a full kinematic physics model (velocity, acceleration, drag, collision flags) modeled after HaxeFlixel's FlxObject.

Most games interact with this through FlixelSprite, which adds graphical capabilities on top of this spatial foundation.

Collision

Use Flixel.overlap() and Flixel.collide() for overlap/separation checks. The static separate(FlixelObject, FlixelObject) method resolves overlaps by adjusting positions and velocities.

  • Field Summary

    active, alive, cameras, exists, ID, visible

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Updates this object's collision bookkeeping and, when moves is true, calls updateMotion(float).
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Overrides:
      update in class FlixelBasic
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds since the last frame.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected. A destroyed FlixelBasic should not be used anymore. Use FlixelBasic.kill() if you only want to disable it temporarily and FlixelBasic.revive() it later.

      Override this function to clean up any resources used by this object, such as textures, fonts, sounds, etc.

      This function is called automatically when FlixelBasic.dispose() or FlixelBasic.reset() is executed, so you don't need to call it manually.

      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelBasic
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()

    • isTouching

      public boolean isTouching(int direction)
      Returns true if this object is currently touching the given surface(s). Flags are set by Flixel.collide(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic, java.util.function.BiConsumer<me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject>) and reset each frame.
      Parameters:
      direction - One or more direction flags ORed together.

    • justTouched

      public boolean justTouched(int direction)
      Returns true if this object just started touching the given surface(s) this frame (was not touching last frame).
      Parameters:
      direction - One or more direction flags ORed together.

    • reset

      public void reset(float x, float y)
      Resets this object to a new position, reviving it and clearing physics state.
      Parameters:
      x - New X position.
      y - New Y position.

    • overlaps

      public boolean overlaps(FlixelObject other)
      Checks if this object's AABB overlaps another FlixelObject.
      Parameters:
      other - The other object.
      Returns:
      true if the two AABBs overlap.

    • overlapsPoint

      public boolean overlapsPoint(float px, float py)
      Checks if a point in world space overlaps this object's AABB.
      Parameters:
      px - X coordinate.
      py - Y coordinate.
      Returns:
      true if the point is inside.

    • inWorldBounds

      public boolean inWorldBounds()
      Returns true if the object is within the world bounds configured on the global manager class.

    • updateMotion

      public void updateMotion(float elapsed)
      Internal function for updating the position and speed of this object using a velocity-verlet style integration matching FlxObject.
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.

    • computeVelocity

      public float computeVelocity(float velocity, float acceleration, float drag, float max, float elapsed)
      Internal function for computing velocity after acceleration and drag.
      Parameters:
      velocity - Current velocity.
      acceleration - Acceleration to apply.
      drag - Drag to apply when acceleration is zero.
      max - Maximum allowed velocity.
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.
      Returns:
      Updated velocity.

    • separate

      public static boolean separate(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects by adjusting positions and velocities. Calls separateX(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject) then separateY(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject).
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      Returns:
      true if the objects were overlapping and were separated.
      See Also:
      • FlxObject.separate

    • separateX

      public static boolean separateX(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects on the X axis.
      Returns:
      true if the objects overlapped and were separated on X.

    • separateY

      public static boolean separateY(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Separates two overlapping objects on the Y axis.
      Returns:
      true if the objects overlapped and were separated on Y.

    • computeOverlapX

      public static float computeOverlapX(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2, boolean checkMaxOverlap)
      Computes the overlap between two objects on the X axis and updates their touching flags. Returns 0 if they do not overlap or if their allowCollisions flags disallow collision on the overlapping sides.
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      checkMaxOverlap - Whether to reject overlaps greater than combined movement + SEPARATE_BIAS.
      Returns:
      Signed overlap in pixels (positive means object1's right side penetrates object2's left side).
      See Also:
      • FlxObject.computeOverlapX

    • computeOverlapY

      public static float computeOverlapY(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2, boolean checkMaxOverlap)
      Computes the overlap between two objects on the Y axis and updates their touching flags. Same semantics as computeOverlapX(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelObject, boolean).
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      checkMaxOverlap - Whether to reject overlaps greater than combined movement + SEPARATE_BIAS.
      Returns:
      Signed overlap in pixels (positive means object1's bottom side penetrates object2's top side).
      See Also:
      • FlxObject.computeOverlapY

    • updateTouchingFlags

      public static boolean updateTouchingFlags(FlixelObject object1, FlixelObject object2)
      Checks if two objects overlap and updates their touching flags without separating them.
      Parameters:
      object1 - First object.
      object2 - Second object.
      Returns:
      true if the two objects are touching.

    • getDebugDrawX

      public float getDebugDrawX(FlixelCamera cam)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      X position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw (includes scroll-factor / parallax). Defaults to FlixelDebugDrawable.getDebugX().
      Specified by:
      getDebugDrawX in interface FlixelDebugDrawable
      Parameters:
      cam - The game camera used for this debug pass.

    • getDebugDrawY

      public float getDebugDrawY(FlixelCamera cam)
      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Y position of the debug box in the same world space as FlixelCamera projection during draw. Defaults to FlixelDebugDrawable.getDebugY().
      Specified by:
      getDebugDrawY in interface FlixelDebugDrawable
      Parameters:
      cam - The game camera used for this debug pass.

    • getDebugHeight

      Description copied from interface: FlixelDebugDrawable
      Height of the bounding box in world pixels.
      Specified by:
      getDebugHeight in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • getDebugBoundingBoxColor

      public float[] getDebugBoundingBoxColor()
      Returns the appropriate debug color based on collision state:
      • Override color if set
      • Blue when allowCollisions == NONE
      • Green when immovable or partial collision
      • Red (normal/solid) otherwise
      Specified by:
      getDebugBoundingBoxColor in interface FlixelDebugDrawable

    • setDebugBoundingBoxColor

      public void setDebugBoundingBoxColor(float[] rgba)
      Forces a specific debug bounding box color, overriding the automatic selection. Pass null to re-enable automatic color selection.
      Parameters:
      rgba - 4-element float array [r, g, b, a], or null.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class FlixelBasic
